results.possibleIdentity: Peter J. Sprague (born circa 1939) is a prominent American entrepreneur, venture capitalist, and business executive who played a pivotal role in the semiconductor industry during the late 1960s and 1970s. He is best known for serving as the Chairman of the Board of National Semiconductor Corporation for approximately 30 years, beginning in 1966 when he was just 27 years old. Under his leadership, the company was transformed into a global industry leader through the strategic hiring of top executives from Fairchild Semiconductor, including Charles E. Sporck. Beyond electronics, Sprague was deeply involved in the New York design and art scenes; he served as a director and investor for Design Research Incorporated (D/R), the influential retail firm founded by Ben Thompson that championed the 'total environment concept' of lifestyle merchandising. His technical and business interests spanned marketing, manufacturing (the subject of his graduate research), and international development, including food technology projects in Iran. Sprague was also a key figure in the rescue and chairmanship of the British automaker Aston Martin in the mid-1970s. He has been associated with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization as a supporter and trustee, reflecting his career-long interest in the intersection of technical innovation and creative design. In 1970, he ran for the U.S. Congress in New York's 17th District.
